elements.text:
Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

This figure shows a map of the United States displaying basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures. The map includes contour lines with wind speed values in both mi/h and m/s (in parentheses), with crosshatched areas indicating special wind regions where location-specific basic wind speeds should be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.

[Structured: {"type":"map","geography":"Continental United States with state boundaries and county lines","parameter":"Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings","contour_values":["100(45)","105(47)","110(49)","115(51)","117(52)","120(54)","130(58)","140(63)","150(67)","160(72)","170(76)","175(78)"],"special_regions":"Crosshatched areas indicating locations requiring ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase consultation"}]
elements.caption: Figure 26.5-1D. Basic wind speeds for Risk Category IV buildings and other structures.

elements.text:
Notes:
1. Values are 3 s gust wind speeds in mi/h (m/s) at 33 ft (10 m) above ground for Exposure Category C.
2. Linear interpolation is permitted between contours. Point values are provided to aid with interpolation.
3. Islands, coastal areas, and land boundaries outside the last contour shall use the last wind speed contour.
4. Location-specific basic wind speeds shall be permitted to be determined using the ASCE Wind Design Geodatabase.
